text:
Once upon a time, there was an old oak tree.

The tree was beautiful. Its branches stretched gracefully into the sky. Its leaves shimmered with an otherworldly golden glow.

One day, an old beggar sat beneath the tree.

He spoke kindly to the tree, telling it stories of his childhood adventures.

The tree listened patiently.

Over time, the old beggar grew closer to the tree.

He spoke secrets of his heart.

The tree listened attentively.

One day, the old man told the tree he wished it could talk.

But the tree did not wish to talk:

Its leaves swayed gently.

It listened.

And heard nothing.

The old man felt disappointed.

One day, the old woman who lived beneath the tree walked out.

She noticed him.

She spoke kindly.

You do speak.

The old man smiled.

The two became friends.

Together they exchanged stories.

And the tree listened.

For the first time.

And finally understood.

It spoke happily ever after.

And
